title: helmet({ frameguard: false }) — X-Frame-Options is off, exposing the app to clickjacking

params.passDetail: helmet keeps frameguard on (frameguard is not false) — an X-Frame-Options header is sent that stops the app being embedded in a hostile frame.
params.failDetail: helmet is configured with frameguard: false, which removes the X-Frame-Options header. Any site can now embed this app in an invisible iframe and trick a logged-in user into clicking hidden controls (clickjacking) — approving actions they never intended. Leave frameguard enabled (helmet's default DENY/SAMEORIGIN).
params.absentCallDetail: No helmet() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: helmet() does not set frameguard; the default (X-Frame-Options enabled) applies.

provenance.note: frameguard: false is set so the app can be embedded in a partner's iframe, but scoped to the whole app it reopens clickjacking everywhere. Nothing breaks visibly, so it ships.
